summary: This draft PR adds BivarColorbar and MultivarColorbar classes to support bivariate/multivariate colormaps alongside significant refactoring of the colorbar axis creation helpers. The feature is new and clearly still in draft state (design questions open, 'needs rebase' label). Core functionality is present with baseline images and tests, but there are several correctness bugs, breaking API changes without deprecation, missing docstrings, missing stubs, and leftover debug/commented-out code that must all be addressed before merge.

issues.comment: Colorizer.get_clim() return type is silently changed for scalar norms. Previously returned `(vmin, vmax)` as plain floats; now returns `((vmin,), (vmax,))` (tuples of length 1). Any user code doing `vmin, vmax = mappable.colorizer.get_clim()` will now unpack single-element tuples instead of floats, causing subtle downstream bugs (e.g., comparisons, arithmetic). This is a breaking public API change that requires the two-stage deprecation protocol (add a FutureWarning in this release, change behavior in 3.N+2) and a doc/api/next_api_changes/behavior_changes/ RST entry.

Colorizer.clip property return type changed from a scalar bool to a 1-tuple `(self.norm.clip,)` for scalar norms. Same issue as get_clim — this silently breaks existing user code that reads `colorizer.clip` and treats it as a bool (e.g., `if colorizer.clip:`). This is an undeprecated public API break.

MultivarColorbar.remove() references `self.ax` (line ~437 in the added code), but the MultivarColorbar class has no `self.ax` attribute — it uses `self.axes` (a list). This will raise an AttributeError at runtime whenever remove() is called. Fix: replace `self.ax` with appropriate logic using `self.axes`.

BivarColorbar.update_normals() has `#self._draw_all()` commented out. This means when the linked mappable fires its 'changed' callback (e.g., after norm/cmap update), the bivariate colorbar will NOT redraw. The colorbar will show stale colors. Either call `self._draw_all()` unconditionally or restore the intended logic.

Mutable default argument `ticklocations=['auto', 'auto']` in BivarColorbar.__init__. Python evaluates default argument values once at function definition time; sharing the same list object across calls can cause hard-to-diagnose bugs. Use an immutable default instead: `ticklocations=('auto', 'auto')` (tuple) or `ticklocations=None` with an internal default.

Large blocks of code are commented out using triple-quoted strings (acting as doc-string literals, not actual comments): the _colorbar_xax_cid/_colorbar_yax_cid block and the two _y_limits_changed/_x_limits_changed/_update_limits blocks. These dead-code blocks suggest unfinished work and must be removed (or converted to a GitHub issue/TODO) before merge.

MultivarColorbar class and its __init__ method have no docstring at all. All public classes in lib/matplotlib/ require a NumPy-style docstring with at least a summary, Parameters, and Notes sections. This is enforced by the D (pydocstyle) ruff rules.
Typo in _bivar_colormap_kw_doc interpd string: `{'auto', 'top', 'bottom''}` has a double single-quote at the end (`bottom''`). This will appear verbatim in rendered documentation.

Missing space in error message string concatenation: `"colorbar can only be together with a" + "scalar colormap"` produces `"colorbar can only be together with ascalar colormap"`. Add a leading space to the second string: `" scalar colormap"`.

Figure.multivar_colorbar() has no docstring. A new public method requires a full NumPy-style docstring with Parameters, Returns, and an Examples section per the skill guidelines. The bivar_colorbar method above it has a docstring as a good template.

MultivarColorbar is not stubbed in colorbar.pyi. The stub file has BivarColorbar but MultivarColorbar (which is public API exported from colorbar.py) is missing entirely. mypy/stubtest CI will fail, and IDE type checking will be broken for users of this class.
Figure.multivar_colorbar() / SubFigure.multivar_colorbar() is missing from figure.pyi. Only bivar_colorbar was added. multivar_colorbar is a new public method and must be stubbed.

The guard `if isinstance(mappable.cmap, mcolors.BivarColormap)` / `isinstance(mappable.cmap, mcolors.MultivarColormap)` added to Figure.colorbar() is a new behavior change: it now raises ValueError for mappables using BivarColormap/MultivarColormap. Users calling fig.colorbar() on a bivariate imshow will get a new error instead of the old (potentially wrong) behavior. This requires a doc/api/next_api_changes/behavior_changes/ RST entry.

No what's-new RST file found for this PR. BivarColorbar, Figure.bivar_colorbar, Figure.multivar_colorbar, and pyplot.bivar_colorbar are all significant new public API additions. Per the contribution guide, a file must be added under doc/release/next_whats_new/ with a versionadded:: directive documenting the new feature.

test_bivar_colorbar_location is parametrized only with `use_gridspec=True` (`@pytest.mark.parametrize('use_gridspec', [True])`). The sister test test_bivar_colorbar_sharing correctly tests both True and False. The gridspec=False path for location testing is not exercised. Expand to `[True, False]` and add a second baseline image.

ColorizingArtist.get_clim() now raises AttributeError (not a more appropriate TypeError or ValueError) when n_components > 1. Using AttributeError implies the attribute doesn't exist, which is misleading. Consider raising TypeError with a clear message, or use _api.check_isinstance to produce a consistent error style.

In the pcolor refactor, `mcolorizer.ColorizingArtist._check_exclusionary_keywords(colorizer, vmin=vmin, vmax=vmax, norm=norm, cmap=cmap)` is called before building the Colorizer. Then `collection._scale_norm(norm, vmin, vmax)` is called after construction. Since the Colorizer is already built with norm/cmap and the collection is passed `colorizer=colorizer` (without separate norm/cmap), calling `_scale_norm(norm, vmin, vmax)` after the fact with potentially non-None values can double-apply scaling. Verify this does not produce incorrect vmin/vmax on the final image.

Minor docstring typo: `"but optional for the`.pyplot.bivar_colorbar` function"` is missing a space before the backtick cross-reference: should be `"but optional for the `.pyplot.bivar_colorbar` function"`.
Double blank line added after `_log = logging.getLogger(__name__)` (two consecutive blank lines where one suffices). Minor style issue but PEP 8 / ruff will likely flag it.

The closing parenthesis of BivarColorbar.__init__ is indented with 16 spaces to align with the `*,` above it, creating unusual indentation. The conventional style for closing parentheses of function signatures in this codebase is to align at the `def` column or 4-space indent. Consider reformatting for consistency.

get_scaled_dtype is defined as a nested function inside _make_image but it operates only on its argument (no closure variables). Consider hoisting it to module level or making it a private module-level helper to improve readability and testability.
